LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INDUSTRIAL BOLT & SUPPLY, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE FAILS TO ESTABLISH SUFFICIENT FACTS FOR THE EXERCISE OF GENERAL JURISDICTION. SUFFICIENT FACTS HAVE BEEN ALLEGED TO ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR LIMITED JURISDICTION. HOWEVER, TO EXERCISE SUCH JURISDICTION IN THIS CASE OFFENDS THE NOTION OF FAIR PLAY AND SUBSTANTIAL JUSTICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Hearing On Defendant Industrial Bolt & Supply, Inc.'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar May-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT INDUSTRIAL BOLT & SUPPLY, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION.
APPEARANCES: BRIAN GAFFNEY, ESQUIRE (LAW OFFICES OF BRIAN GAFFNEY, 415.442.0711) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. KARLEEN M. O'CONNOR, ESQUIRE (STOVEL RIVES LLP, 415.617.8965) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE FAILS TO ESTABLISH SUFFICIENT FACTS FOR THE EXERCISE OF GENERAL JURISDICTION. SUFFICIENT FACTS HAVE BEEN ALLEGED TO ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR LIMITED JURISDICTION. HOWEVER, TO EXERCISE SUCH JURISDICTION IN THIS CASE OFFENDS THE NOTION OF FAIR PLAY AND SUBSTANTIAL JUSTICE.
